source.title: Limbo - Travis MacBeth (Toreador) Chris Dassaro (3.5.1)

Appearance - +3 Social on Non Power uses dealing with focus.
Perception - +3 Mental on Non Power uses dealing with focus.

rows:
*Transcript of interview November 20, 2015 – Destroy after reading*

Alright so where should I start?
Beginning always seems to be a good spot.
OK. well I was born August 15 19..
Lets specify a little bit, I don't care about your life story.  I'm more interested in your, un-life as it were.
Oh, well then lets jump to '56.  I'd only been back in country for about 2 or 3 months when I first meet her.  There was this bar, the Dirty Piston, where all the mechanics hung out.
Back from where?
Korea.  There was a war there.
Yeah I heard about it, why where you there?
I was in the Army, as a mechanic.  Thought you didn't care about that stuff.
I'll tell you what I do or don't care about.  That means combat experience which might come in handy.
Well combat training at any rate.  Mostly survival skills are the only thing the army trained me in that I've really used.  But getting back to my story.  
This chick was, as I remember, gorgeous.  I'd seen her in there a few times, talking to some of the guys.  This time, it was me she wanted to talk to.  
What about?
My experience with customization and fabrication.  I'd thought it just did it for her cause before I knew it we were at her place, but not for the reasons I expected.  I was a bit drunk and blacked out so my memory of the actual event is well, not there. I woke up  the next night changed, as I'm sure you figured.
She didn't stay long that night, or really explain much.  Just said something about Toreador, clans, and to continue my passions.  Didn't think much of it, until I started to get hungry.
Ah yes, memories.  What was her name?
She told me Marilyn, but I always thought that was a lie, just didn't look like a Marilyn ya know?  Not that it mattered, I only ever saw her once more about 6 months later.  Hows that for a fuck you?  She showed up one night at the shop i'd been working at.
I told the owner that I was on medication that wouldn't allow me to be in the sunlight, so he let me work at night. 
Anyways.  Here she comes, throws two giant duffle bags at me and tells me to hide.  Doesn't even tell me why, then disappears.  Never saw her again.  
What was in the Bags?
Money, and lots of it.  Enough that I was able to buy me a Garage, and do a little, customization so I could live there.  Halo Hot Rods, it's where you found me.  So that's pretty much what I did.  Worked the shop at night slept during the day.  Worked well, no one found me. Assuming there was someone even looking and that chick wasn't just batshit.  
Worked well too,  until the early 80's.
What happened then?  Other than the death of fashion?
I was found.  Though I don't think he expected what he'd found.  
Working late one night I heard a bike pull up.  We had people come by late because they knew someone would be there, but this guy.  Red he said his name was.  Big Grizzly of a man.  And I mean that, hairiest fucker I'd ever seen.  Told me he'd heard about my skills and he wanted me to customize his Harley Panhead.  Told him I mostly worked on cars, but he insisted.  When I named my price he laughed and told me I didn't know who I was messing with.  I guess he thought I was human because he swung at me and the look on his face when my counter punch sent him flying across the room was priceless.  He stood up baring fangs and claws.  
Well I bared the fangs back and grabbed 2 large torque wrenches.  He just stopped and started laughing.  He said he may not have money, but he could show me a few things that would help me out in exchange for the bike.  Honestly I didn't care about the money, i'd never worked on a Panhead and wanted the challenge, so I said yes.  
What clan was he? Though I'm pretty sure I can guess.
He said Gangrel.
Yup, points to me.  Though it seems odd that he didn't realize you were a vamp. They have a pretty good sense of smell.  
Don't know what to tell ya.  Doing that Panhead really worked out.  See he was actually a member of a Hells Angels nomad Chapter.  So I started getting bikers in the shop to get their rides customized.  Even go invited to a few parties.  
Only ever went to one.  Man was that a bad idea.
I'll bite, what happened?
Well nothing at first, spent the early part doing some minor repairs and alterations to a few of my creations.  You know, re-align this re-position that.  About midnight or so, there was some kind of ruckus because Red told me to stay at the club house and they'd be back.  Only about 3 bikers stayed and the rest bailed.  
Now I should mention that there had been some families at this thing, the kids had go to bed earlier, bit the women were still wondering around.  That is until everyone left.  The women disappeared too .  Didn't think too much of it, until that molotov came in through the window.  Man I saw fire and I bailed.  And by that I mean I ran upstairs.  Seems the original activity was a distraction to get the bikers to leave.  Some rival gang was targeting the families.  
I didn't see them come in, but man did I hear them.  There were about 30 or so that stormed the clubhouse.  They made short work of the three angels that had stayed.  As they came charging up the stairs I realized that unlike the downstairs, the upstairs was built like a fortress.  The doors were all locked and steal.  I had no where to go, when they saw me.
*PAUSE*
Well?  Now I'm interested, you cant stop there.  Out with the details.
Red had been killed that night, guess someone got him with some military grade flammables.   Found out when the other bikers returned. They found me, upstairs, covered in blood.  The bodies of about 15 of the attackers shredded around me.  They had all been human, easy prey.  
They cheered and congratulated me.  I hadn't known it at the time, but the families had been hiding behind those doors, and the attackers had had explosives.
 Ever since I've had security around my shop, supplied by the angels.  One of them, a kid that goes by the name Bear, feeds me info on the happenings around town, and helps out if I need things.  Seems he was one of the kids behind those doors.  
Isn't that a touching story.
Well, it wasn't intentional but it's helped me out a bunch.  After that every things been quiet.  A few gang wars recently.  Though I guess that's why you guys are here.  Haven't been bothered or found out by anyone else.  Until you walked in that is.  And so, here we are.
Here we are indeed.  Well, there are going to be a few things we shall have to take care of, but you have been brought into the fold and have the Clans protection to go along with your bikers.  
Well hopefully it'll be for the best.  Can I go now?  I got this '57 Plymouth I need to finish for the auto show next week...
Yes yes, we know where to find you.  Oh wait.  You know I just realized you never said your name.
Didn't I?  Hmm?  My name is Travis MacBeth.
Your relationship to these people is described. This may mean they do not know you.
